Fears over a new wave of inflation today sent the UK Government's borrowing costs to the highest levels since 2008 - the peak of the credit crunch misery. As the Duchess of Sussex beamed and hugged her best friend Kelly Zajfen at a charity bash in LA last night (right), missing from her finger was her dazzling diamond engagement ring and eternity band. I know a man who lost his company and then his wife had an affair and divorced him. They had 3 young kids. He had to find a job asap (to provide for his family) so he had to move 3 states away. He was going through a divorce, living far from his kids, and living in a state where he knew no one. He was really depressed, as he adored his kids and wife. Because his wife divorced him when he was bankrupt, she didn't get a lot of money. She and her lover eventually broke up. She's broke now, living paycheck to paycheck in an apartment. Her ex worked his way back up to executive level job at a huge corporation and his kids have chosen to live with him. My point is, give it time...things level out eventually.

Paul and Lisa Toomer, who live in an affluent suburb of Poole, Dorset, erected the large looming wooden fence due to concerns about their security and privacy. The couple, who bought the house for over £1million five years ago, witnessed a spate of attempted burglaries and after a new home was built on the road behind -  which could see directly into their property - they decided to take actions. In response, they put up a 6.5ft wooden fence at the bottom of their garden, thinking they were within their rights under permitted development. But since then the couple have been locked in a £3,000 battle with Bournemouth, Christchurch and Poole Council (BCP) who have demanded that the fence be torn down.

NEW This is the shocking moment a pensioner was brutally attacked on his own driveway by a masked thug with a wooden plank. CCTV footage shows Joe Storer, 74, being struck in the face while he checked his bins outside his home in Derby. Mr Storer opens the main gate before a man, dressed in black and wearing a ski mask, charges at him with a plank of wood riddled with nails. The GOAT.

Ronnie O'Sullivan celebrated a 'pretty cool moment' after making the highest break in professional snooker by hitting a 153 at the World Open on Friday morning. The 50-year-old achieved the unprecedented feat after leaving quarter-final opponent Ryan Day in a snooker at the start of the opening frame and the Welshman's failed attempt to get out of it handed O'Sullivan a free ball. That allowed the seven-time world champion to start his break with the green - for one point - followed by his opening black to put him on eight points before even beginning on the reds.

  • Catherine and William are a huge asset to the UK. Catherine looks lovely but please stop with the comparisons. It’s more than time to let Diana go, she was a style icon at the time but the fashion was not always great in the time when she was part of the RF.
  • I used to enjoy it, but now I feel like I need an insulin shot after watching. It's become Hallmark on steroids. Enough with Hope and Doc making out, Jack has become so sappy and looks like he needs a shave and a hair wash. Very disappointed.
  • As always, still as crude and as common as muck.
  • My kid once wrote a letter to him as he was a big fan of his books. Williams wrote back a handwritten note thanking him and assuring him he too could write, all he needed was a pen and paper. He included a pen with the note. I thought it was a very classy move
  • My thoughts are with these young people and their families. An awful situation that appears to have happened at random with little explanation.
  • Theres something rotten at the very heart of the BBC, and they still don’t see it
  • The Exorcist doesn't need a reboot!! Stop ruining classic films!!
  • As soon as I saw three of the names, Lenny Henry, Sturgeon and Aitch, I knew it was another programme to add to my "Must Avoid" list.
  • Wishing him as pain free a journey as possible
  • Not that Jane will ever read or see this … my terminally ill mother loved watching Jane’s shows, I would sit and watch with her. Every week, Jane brought some light into my Mum’s world. I’ll miss those evenings. Thank you Jane.
  • Another non story
  • she really does look like him. hope she is happy and doing ok!
  • Invictus needs to drop them next. She spends hundreds of thousands on hotels, clothing, hair & makeup, security and Range Rovers... money that should have been spent on the veterans transportation and accommodations. Please protest Invictus.
  • Yes! love this show!!
  • What's with all this "beloved" tripe? Can't you just say popular?
  • This Life and the great Daniela Nardini, she and Helen Baxendale were my favourite actresses back then.....what happened to them both?

  • Well at least he thinks like 90% of the UK that uneducated kids getting engaged is not a wise move.
  • Well now that blends in seamlessly with the rest of the Cornish cottages there...
  • That dress is beautiful on her. The new hairstyle looks really classy as well.
  • What a stunner Liz is for sixty!
  • Vapid. Advertising her bought wares for any other sap now that Dimothee is not flavour of the month.
  • I'm sorry she lost her family members in such short order. I would suggest it would be prudent to use the alimony/child support money to actually support her girls and stop using the fillers for the duck lips.
  • So she’s come running back after the big wedding, so she can get free childcare from granny
  • I got as far as Kixee Moon and had to stop as I was laughing so much my phone was shaking. Is that pronounced as kick see? That poor child
  • Good! No one wants to see these old has beens

  • I believe that. Can't believe she has any fans.

  • As someone who was a massive Buffy and Angel fan back in the day, I'm glad they've scrapped the revival. Leave these shows alone. Reboots literally never work.

  • Why are we beating up JFK Jr? He's dead and can't defend himself. Can't you find anyone else who's alive to write about?
  • He's good looking now. But he was absolutely stunning back then

Bruce Willis seen with granddaughter in touching photos shared by ex-wife Demi Moore on his 71st birthday amid dementia battle

Demi Moore shared rare photos of Bruce Willis bonding with his granddaughter in celebration of his 71st birthday. The 63-year-old actress - who recently showed off her slimmer frame at the Vanity Fair Oscars party - jumped to her main Instagram page on Thursday to pen a tribute to the star as he turned one year older. It comes three years after his family emotionally announced his frontotemporal dementia diagnosis.
  • 152 comments
  • 2 videos
  • share Bruce Willis seen with granddaughter in photos shared by ex Demi Moore

First look at AI-generated Val Kilmer after controversial decision to bring back late star for new film

The first image of AI-generated Val Kilmer has been released as late actor is set to be brought back for one final role a year after this death.  The Hollywood star died at the age of 65 on April 1, 2025 after a battle with throat cancer but had already signed on to star in As Deep as the Grave.  Kilmer was unable to film because of his health, so filmmakers decided to use AI to see Kilmer portray Catholic priest and Native American spiritualist Father Fintan.
  • 123 comments
  • 1 video
  • share First look at AI-generated Val Kilmer as late star appears in new film
